expectations.text: CreateTaskTool extends FunctionCallBase (from Drupal\ai\Base\FunctionCallBase) not AiFunctionCallBase (the existing CreateProjectTool in the codebase extends Drupal\ai_agents\Plugin\AiFunctionCall\AiFunctionCallBase which is a non-existent class -- without skill guidance, Haiku copies this broken pattern from existing code instead of using the correct base class from the installed AI module)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php line 38: 'class CreateTaskTool extends FunctionCallBase implements ExecutableFunctionCallInterface' with use statement 'use Drupal\ai\Base\FunctionCallBase;' - uses correct base class

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool is discovered via #[FunctionCall] PHP attribute (from Drupal\ai\Attribute\FunctionCall), not via @AiFunctionCall docblock annotation (the existing CreateProjectTool uses @AiFunctionCall annotation syntax -- without the module-scaffold skill teaching PHP 8 attributes as the preferred discovery mechanism, Haiku copies the annotation pattern from existing tools, which is the outdated API)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php line 17-37: Has '#[FunctionCall(id: 'create_task_tool', ...)]' PHP attribute with use statement 'use Drupal\ai\Attribute\FunctionCall;'

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool defines its inputs ('text' and 'project_id') via context_definitions array in the #[FunctionCall] attribute, not via a getArguments() method (the existing CreateProjectTool uses getArguments() which is the old annotation-era pattern -- without the plugins-blocks skill teaching ContextDefinition, Haiku copies getArguments(), preventing the AI chatbot from knowing what arguments the tool accepts)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php lines 23-36: Has 'context_definitions: ["text" => new ContextDefinition(...), "project_id" => new ContextDefinition(...)]' in the #[FunctionCall] attribute - no getArguments() method present

expectations.text: CreateTaskTool accesses input values via $this->getContextValue('text') and $this->getContextValue('project_id'), not via $this->functionCall->getArgumentsObject()->text (the old accessor pattern from CreateProjectTool; getContextValue() is the context-aware plugin system method that corresponds to context_definitions declared in the #[FunctionCall] attribute)

CreateTaskTool create() method has 4 parameters: create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) -- NOT the controller-style 1-parameter create(ContainerInterface $container) (the plugins-blocks skill explicitly teaches the CRITICAL plugin DI pattern with 4 parameters; Haiku frequently confuses controller DI with plugin DI because both use a method named create() but with incompatible signatures that cause a fatal error when the plugin manager calls it)
expectations.evidence: CreateTaskTool.php line 50: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition): FunctionCallInterface|static' - correct 4-parameter signature

expectations.text: AiTaskService is registered in group_ai_pm.services.yml with @?ai.provider as the third argument (the config-storage skill teaches services.yml registration with DI; the @? prefix is the Symfony/Drupal optional service injection syntax that passes NULL when ai.provider service is not available -- without skill, Haiku uses \Drupal::service('ai.provider') or makes it a required dependency, causing a fatal error when the AI module is not installed)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.services.yml: Service registered as 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' (not 'group_ai_pm.ai_task') with '@?ai.chat' as third argument (not '@?ai.provider'). Service ID mismatch and wrong optional service name.

expectations.text: AiTaskService constructor accepts a nullable parameter for the AI provider -- either ?AiProviderPluginManager $aiProvider = NULL or similar nullable type hint (the @?ai.provider in services.yml injects NULL when AI module is absent; without a nullable parameter declaration, PHP throws a type error when NULL is injected into a non-nullable typed parameter)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskService.php lines 46-53: Constructor has third parameter '$ai_chat = NULL' (defaulting to NULL). The parameter is typed as nullable with 'ChatInterface|null $ai_chat' in the docblock.

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes _csrf_request_header_token: 'TRUE' in its requirements, NOT _csrf_token: 'TRUE' (the access-security skill and routing-controllers skill both teach the CRITICAL distinction: _csrf_token validates tokens in the URL query string for server-rendered links, while _csrf_request_header_token validates the X-CSRF-Token request header for JavaScript fetch() callers -- using _csrf_token on API routes causes 403 errors for all JavaScript POST requests)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml lines 40-41: 'requirements:\n    _permission: 'access group_ai_pm kanban'\n    _csrf_request_header_token: 'TRUE'' - correct header-based CSRF protection

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes _format: json in its requirements section (the routing-controllers skill explicitly teaches this pattern with CRITICAL callout: without _format:json, Drupal returns HTML error pages for 403/404/422 instead of JSON -- JavaScript callers receive unparseable HTML instead of a structured error object)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml: '_format: json' appears in the 'defaults' section (line 37), NOT in the 'requirements' section. This is incorrect placement - _format must be in requirements to control error response format.

expectations.text: The ai_create route uses entity upcasting via options.parameters.project.type: entity:project (the routing-controllers skill has a CRITICAL callout for custom entity types: without explicit upcasting config, Drupal may pass a raw ID string instead of a loaded Project entity object to the controller method, causing a type error when the controller calls entity methods)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml: No 'options.parameters.project.type: entity:project' present. The route has only 'options: _admin_route: TRUE' with no entity upcasting configuration.

expectations.text: The ai_create route includes options._admin_route: TRUE (the routing-controllers skill has a CRITICAL callout that routes under /admin/ do NOT automatically inherit the admin theme -- without _admin_route: TRUE, this route renders with the frontend theme; this is consistently missed without the skill)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml lines 43-44: 'options:\n    _admin_route: TRUE' - correct admin route marking

expectations.text: AiTaskController uses a create() factory method with proper DI to inject AiTaskService from the container, NOT the lazy entityTypeManager() or currentUser() helper methods from ControllerBase for loading entities (the routing-controllers skill marks static \Drupal:: calls as the #1 DI violation and teaches explicit create()+constructor injection; without skill, Haiku may skip create() and use \Drupal::service() or lazy helpers)
expectations.evidence: AiTaskController.php lines 39-43: 'public static function create(ContainerInterface $container) { return new static($container->get("group_ai_pm.ai_task_service")); }' - proper DI factory method

expectations.text: Config schema file group_ai_pm.schema.yml includes ai_model in its mapping with type: string (the config-storage skill teaches that every config key needs a schema entry -- the existing schema has ai_provider but not ai_model; without skill, Haiku adds the functional code to read ai_model but omits the schema, which breaks config export validation)
expectations.evidence: config/schema/group_ai_pm.schema.yml: Has 'ai_model:\n  type: string\n  label: 'AI Model'' in the mapping section

expectations.text: Config install file group_ai_pm.settings.yml includes ai_model with a default value (the config-storage skill teaches that config/install/ files provide defaults so the config object exists from module installation; without the default, code reading ai_model gets NULL until someone saves the settings form)
expectations.evidence: config/install/group_ai_pm.settings.yml line 4: 'ai_model: ''' - has ai_model key with empty string default

expectations.text: group_ai_pm.services.yml exists and registers a service (group_ai_pm.ai_task or group_ai_pm.ai_task_service) with class Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService, and the service ID is used consistently across services.yml, controller create() factory, and plugin create() factory (this is wiring: the service must be discoverable from the container; inconsistent service IDs cause ServiceNotFoundException at runtime)
expectations.evidence: group_ai_pm.services.yml registers 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' with class Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService. Controller create() uses $container->get('group_ai_pm.ai_task_service') and plugin create() uses $container->get('group_ai_pm.ai_task_service') — consistent wiring throughout.

runtime_assertions.name: Main module enables cleanly without AI module
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled | grep group_ai_pm
runtime_assertions.evidence: group_ai_pm module is enabled and cache rebuild completes successfully

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService resolves from service container
runtime_assertions.evidence: Service 'group_ai_pm.ai_task_service' resolves successfully from container (note: both variants used ai_task_service not ai_task as the service ID)

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService isAvailable() returns false when AI module not configured
runtime_assertions.evidence: isAvailable() returns false - AI chat service is NULL (no AI module configured)

runtime_assertions.name: CreateTaskTool plugin is discovered by function calls plugin manager
runtime_assertions.evidence: Plugin with ID containing 'create_task' found in plugin.manager.ai.function_calls definitions

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route has _format:json requirement
runtime_assertions.evidence: _format is in route defaults, not requirements. The requirement check returns FAIL.

runtime_assertions.name: Config schema includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.evidence: config.typed returns schema with ai_model in mapping

runtime_assertions.name: Config install default includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php:script rt12.php (after manual cset)
runtime_assertions.evidence: config/install/group_ai_pm.settings.yml contains ai_model: '' - present on fresh install
